mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) reflect expanding output and real economic activity, which drives corporate earnings growth and lifts equity valuations, predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO growth as a predictor of 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any trailing 2-year window in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 0.5% after a prolonged period of near-zero rates, it signals the start of a tightening cycle that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.5% after being below 0.25% for at least 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: More than 50% of trigger instances yield positive 20-day forward returns, or the average excess return after trigger is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in real retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) over a three-month period signals broad-based weakening in consumer and investment demand, which foreshadows declining corporate revenues and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: 3-month growth in RSAFS is negative and 3-month growth in HOUST is negative by more than 5%
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce negative 60-day forward returns in at least 70% of occurrences during out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime defined by rising federal funds rate, the housing sector (HOUST) is especially sensitive to higher borrowing costs; a decline in housing starts under these conditions signals weakening economic activity and predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle, high interest rate sensitivity
failure_condition: In identified tightening regimes, the correlation between HOUST month-over-month change and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not negative with statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below the threshold of 4.5% indicates a tightening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 4.5% within a rolling 3-month window
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return after trigger is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the hit rate (probability of positive return) is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When growth in industrial production (INDPRO) outpaces growth in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) over a three-month period, it signals real economic expansion without overheating, which boosts corporate profitability and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month percentage change exceeds CPIAUCSL 3-month percentage change by at least 0.5 percentage points
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO growth minus CPIAUCSL growth) has a 60-day forward return Rank IC below 0.04 over any trailing 3-year period in walk-forward analysis.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation regime (CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth below 3%), increases in real retail sales (RSAFS) serve as a strong near-term catalyst for equity markets, as consumer spending signals economic vitality without erosion of purchasing power, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: In low inflation periods, the correlation between RSAFS weekly changes and 5-day forward returns is not positive (r <= 0) or the average forward return is not positive with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: A positive quarterly growth rate in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects robust aggregate demand that drives corporate earnings growth over a longer horizon, predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The predictive power of GDPC1 quarterly growth on 60-day forward returns has an R-squared below 1% in out-of-sample regression, or the directional accuracy is less than 55%.
